observations.recommendation:
1.10 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant: (1) Compute  the  monthly
depreciation of all Infrastructure Asset,  and;  (2)  record  the  necessary
adjustments on the unrecognized depreciation expenses in  order  to  reflect
the correct carrying value of Infrastructure Assets  in  the  books  of  the
LGU.

2.8  We recommended that the Municipal Accountant: (1) Reclassify  the  cost
of road lot components recorded under Land account to Road Network  account,
and; (2) Reconcile the Local Road Inventory Report with  those  recorded  in
the books.

2.9 We also recommended that the Municipal  Engineer  assist  the  Inventory
Committee in completing the physical count of Local Road Network.

2.10 Lastly, we recommended that the  Municipal  General  Services  Officer:
(1) Complete the reconciliation of his records with that  of  the  Municipal
Accountant and the Inventory Committee; and (2) Maintain  a  complete  Local
Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components and  update  the
same based on the report  of  the  Municipal  Engineer  on  any  repairs  or
removal done.

3.10  We  recommended  that  the  Municipal  Accountant  prepare  correcting
entries to reclassify the cost of installation of grab bars as additions  to
the carrying amount of the building to which these  were  attached  and  the
cost of installation of the  industrial  wall  fan  to  PPE-Other  Equipment
account from the Repair and Maintenance Expense account.

4.6 We recommended that the  Municipal  Accountant  reconcile  these  intra-
agency receivables and payables accounts in the  General  Fund,  Trust  Fund
and Special Education Fund books to reflect  the  correct  balances  of  the
reciprocal accounts.

5.8  We  recommended  that  the  Municipal  Accountant;   (1)   review   the
Disbursement Vouchers on payments to contractors to determine who  has  been
given advances by the LGU, (2) maintain a Subsidiary Ledger  for  individual
contractor per projects to to support the GL balances,  and;  (3)  reconcile
monthly the SL and GL balances.

6.7  We  recommended  that  the  Municipal  Accountant:  (1)  determine  the
composition of the Construction In-Progress  account  balance  by  reviewing
all payments to contractors of infrastructure projects, and;  (2)  maintain
an SL for each on-going project to support the GL balance.

7.10 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:  (1)  submit  immediately
the Fund Utilization Reports (FUR) on funds received from the DAR  and  DILG
and; (2) record the completed projects in the  appropriate  Property,  Plant
and Equipment account so that the liability account in the books of the  LGU
will be closed.

8.6 We recommended that  the  Municipal  Mayor  as  Chairman  of  the  Local
Development Council, appropriate projects for 20% development fund which  is
aligned with the provisions of DILG-DBM JMC 2017-1 dated February  22,  2017
to address the socio-economic and environmental development concerns of  the
LGU’s constituents.

9.11 We recommended that the BAC Chairman and BAC Secretariat  prepare
and  attach  the  General  Conditions  of  the  Contract  and  Special
Conditions of the Contract as integral part of all perfected  Contract
Agreements on Infrastructure Projects implemented.   These  should  be
submitted to COA within five working  days  upon  perfection  together
with all other documents forming part thereof.

10.7 We recommended that the  Municipal  Mayor:  (1)  revoke  the  Executive
Order that designated the MPDC as in-charge of projects in order  to  adhere
with the provisions of RA 544, and (2) require  the Municipal Engineer to
perform  the  functions  defined  in  RA  No.  7160   on   supervising   and
administering engineering works of the LGU so that  only  a  licensed  civil
engineer will sign the designs, plans, and specifications of  LGU  projects.

11.8 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Head  of  MPDC  as  the
designated Project Manager: (1) sign  the  Certificate  of  Acceptance  only
after the one-year defect liability period has elapsed, and (2) require  the
contractors to submit a Performance Bond with coverage period  of  one  year
after the completion of the project.

12.7 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Chairman  of  the  Bids
and Awards Committee (BAC) submit to COA the  documents  pertaining  to  the
procurement and hiring of the consultants of the municipality.

12.8  Also, we  recommended  that  the  Municipal  Accountant  refrain  from
processing payments for consultants unless these  are  duly  supported  with
complete documents to  validate  proper  procurement  process  and  services
rendered.

13.9 We recommended that the  Municipal  Treasurer  update  immediately  the
Real Property Tax Account Register/Taxpayer’s  index  card  and  submit  the
same to the Municipal Accountant at the start of each year.

13.10 We  also  recommended  that  the  Municipal  Accountant  record  every
January of each year the RPT and SET Receivables based on the Real  Property
Tax Account Register/Taxpayer’s Index Card  from  the  Municipal  Treasurer.
Also, tally the balance of the RPT and SET Receivables account  against  the
List of Delinquent Taxpayers prepared by the Municipal Treasurer.

14.10 We recommended that the  Municipal  Treasurer  furnish  the  Municipal
Accountant with a snapshot copy of the Bank Statements every month as  basis
in recording the Municipality’s Share  in  Internal  Revenue  Allotment.  We
also recommended that the Municipal Accountant record  the  LGU’s  share  in
IRA for CY 2018 and every month thereafter.

16.14  We  recommended  that  the  Municipal  Accountant:  (1)  inform   all
Department Heads on the cut-off date for submission of all claims every  end
of the year for inclusion as Accounts Payable and impose  it  strictly;  (2)
decline processing of claims submitted after cut-off date;   (3)  record  as
Accounts Payable only those with  valid  claims  on  the  year  the  related
expenses were incurred, and; (4) record all DVs  processed  and  signed  and
monitor its payments to determine year-end valid payables.

16.15 We also recommended that the  Municipal  Budget  Officer:  (1)  submit
justifications on the delay in forwarding processed  DVs  to  the  Municipal
Treasurer.

2) Stop releasing checks to payees and require the payees to sign the DVs in the designated area within the premises of the Municipal Treasurer’s Office; 3) Exert thorough efforts to search and trace the missing disbursement vouchers, otherwise, all unrecorded paid disbursements will be disallowed in audit; 4) Maintain Check Register to record the date and names of claimants of checks issued.
observations.managementAction: Releasing of checks to payees within the premises of the Mun. Treasurer’s Office was already done. These check disbursements were already recorded in the books. Check register was maintained to record the date and names of claimants of checks issued.

observations.recommendation: 7) We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the Disbursement Voucher to return the unused amounts of completed projects/programs to the source National Government Agency, or to the Bureau of Treasury if the source agency can no longer be traced and to return to the LGU concerned any amount due them.
observations.managementAction: All unused fund s transferred from NGAs were returned to source agencies.

observations.recommendation: 10)We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Planning Officer to implement fully the development projects identified in the Annual Investment Plan of the LGU for the current year pursuant to pertinent provision of DILG-DBM JMC 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017. 11) We also recommended that the Municipal Development Council re-evaluate the necessity of the unimplemented projects in previous years and re-appropriate the funds for other more relevant and currently-needed projects.
observations.managementAction: Although 100% implementatio n of the 20% Development Fund was not achieved, the LGU has implemented more than half of the appropriated development projects during the year.
observations.reason: Due to various implementatio n of funds received from NGAs, the LGU could not undertake implementatio n of other projects. Partially Implemented All unimplemented projects out of 20% DF will be implemented in 2019.

observations.recommendation: 12)We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer coordinate with each other and see to it that the remitted taxes for the month tally with the taxes withheld as per books. Also, the unaccounted discrepancy should be given utmost attention and should be adjusted immediately if necessary or remit the amount to the BIR if determined to be included in the amount due to BIR to avoid incurrence of additional surcharges. Lastly, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer, as the persons primarily responsible for the recording and settlement of the LGU’s tax liabilities, immediately settle the amount due to BIR to avoid incurrence of additional interest, be more religious in paying LGU taxes on or before the due date and check the remittances made by their staff against the returns to ascertain whether the amount paid is actually the amount due.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer were still on the process of determining the discrepancy.
observations.reason: Due to shortage of manpower in the Accounting and Treasurer’s Office, the reconciliatio n could not be done at once.

observations.recommendation: 13) Prepare the Program of Work and Detailed Estimates for the repair of the defects on the project and the pipe connections from the stub out to the individual households; 14) Undertake the repair of the defects on the project using the retention money due to the contractor; 15) Appropriate from the 20% Development Funds the amount for the pipe connections from the stub out to the individual households based on the Program of Work; 16) Make the project operational in CY 2018.
observations.managementAction: The repair of the defects was done by the Contractor. The LGU has appropriated out of the 20% DF for the pipe connections from the stub out to the individual households. The appropriated project was not implemented in 2018.
observations.reason: Not Implemented Due to numerous projects from trust funds implemented

observations.recommendation: 17)We recommended that the Municipal DRRMO maximize the utilization of the LDRRMF in the Trust Fund by including in the LDRRMF Investment Plan programs, projects and activities which will ensure readiness of the LGU in times of calamity.
observations.managementAction: Utilization of the unexpended LDRRM Fund was not maximized in CY 2018.

observations.recommendation: 18)We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer ensure that the appropriate bank account is charged for disaster-related expenses out of the current year’s LDRRMF. 19) We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant make the necessary accounting entries to rectify the same and restore the correct balance of CY 2017 LDRRM fund.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant were on the process of reconciling the Trust Fund account against the LDRRM Fund account to restore the correct balance of the later.

observations.recommendation: 22)We recommended that the Human Resource and Management Officer reconcile their records with the GSIS and communicate immediately to the said agency any necessary change in the employees’ employment status to update the records of GSIS and tally with the LGU records on premium contributions. 23) We also recommended that the Agency Authorized Officer (AO) furnish monthly all payroll preparer with copies of the GSIS billing to ensure that the correct amount of monthly premium contributions and loan amortizations deducted in the payroll are based on the said GSIS billing. 24) Lastly, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconcile monthly the actual deductions in the payroll against the GSIS Billing and against the amount remitted to avoid discrepancies therein.
observations.managementAction: All remittances to GSIS for premium contributions were done. However, existing difference pertain to employees loan remittances. Reconciliation was started by the Accounting Office.
observations.reason: Reconciliatio n will take time due to shortage of personnel in the Accounting and Treasurer’s Office.

observations.recommendation: 25)We recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure that payments of JO wages are supported with the Contracts of Service of each JO to preclude the suspension or disallowance of transactions during post-audit.
observations.managementAction: All payments for wages of JO personnel were covered by Contract of Service.

observations.recommendation: 27)We recommended that the Local School Board incorporate in its budget programs and activities intended for Early Childhood Care Development and allocate one percent (1%) of its IRA in the Annual Budget, as provided.
observations.managementAction: Implementatio n of programs and projects for ECCD were attributed already to other programs like maintenance of Day Care Center, feeding programs and other activities.

observations.recommendation: 28)We recommended that the Mayor as the Chairman of the Municipal Council for the Protection of Children (LCPC) require its members to identify programs, projects and activities for the protection of children’s rights and welfare in the Annual Work and Financial Plan for inclusion in the Annual Budget of the Municipality. The total appropriation of said projects shall be equivalent to one percent (1%) of the IRA in the Annual Budget.
observations.managementAction: The LCPC had identified programs and activities for the protection of children’s rights and welfare in the Annual Work and Financial Plan which was included in the Annual Budget.

observations.recommendation: 29)We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer submit immediately all paid but unrecorded disbursement vouchers to the Municipal Accountant’s Office for recording in the LGUs books of accounts. These disbursement vouchers and its supporting documents must be submitted to COA immediately for audit to avoid suspensions or disallowances in audit. Also, ensure that all DVs are supported with complete supporting documents and bear the signatures of all the approving officials before checks are issued to the claimants to avoid incurring problems on delayed recording and loss of documents. 30) We also recommended that the Municipal accountant record the reconciling items in the Bank Reconciliation Statements, especially those based on the bank statements.
observations.managementAction: Notice of Suspensions issued for the submission of the disbursement transactions pertaining to this observation were settled. The accountant was in the process of locating the documents for these transactions. However, transactions amounting P300,000.00 remained unrecorded. The accountant was in the process of reconciling items in the BRS pertaining to unlocated transactions.
observations.reason: Reconciling items in the BRS pertaining to unlocated transactions were not recorded for lack of basis.

observations.recommendation: 31)We recommended that the Municipal Mayor strictly enforce the liquidation of all cash advances at the end of the year to protect government funds from possible loss or misuse. 32) We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant withhold the salaries of all employees who continuously fail to settle their outstanding cash advances and refrain from granting additional cash advances to those with previous unliquidated balances. 33)We recommended that for the immediate settlement/liquidation of the cash advances by the accountable officers. Demand letter should be issued by the Accountant to the Accountable Officers and enforce the sanctions that are available to the LGU for the collection of the advances. 43) Also, we recommended that management stops the practice of granting cash advances to elected officials for special/other purposes. Cash advances shall be allowed for their official travels only.
observations.managementAction: Liquidation Reports for a cash advance in CY 2018 amounting P213,584.03 by the Mun. Treasurer for SB Payroll and for travel in 2015 amounting P31,000.00 and P134,000.00 remained unlocated. Partial settlement of cash advances was thru deduction from payrolls. Demand letters were sent by the Municipal Accountant. The Municipal Mayor was still granted special cash advance which remained unliquidated to date.
observations.reason: The documents were not found yet.

observations.recommendation: 33)We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer to accelerate the implementation of projects funded by the National Government Agencies (NGAs) and to initiate the return of unutilized funds to the National Treasury if the purpose can no longer be attained.
observations.managementAction: Implementation and liquidation of funds transferred from NGAs were already maximized in CY 2018 although there were still some projects for completion. Return of unutilized funds were already done.

observations.recommendation: 35)We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal DRRMF officer to implement the programs, project and activities in the annual LDRRMF Investment Plan in order to maximize the utilization of the LDRRM fund and achieve fully the LGU’s objective of enhancing the municipality’s disaster preparedness and response capabilities.
observations.managementAction: The appropriation s for LDRRM Fund in CY 2018 was not maximized.

observations.recommendation: 36)We recommended that the Local School Board utilize the SEF fund for school facilities and other priority program s/project/activities and maximize the use of the fund in accordance with the intended purpose in order to obtain the desired benefits.
observations.managementAction: Utilization of SEF in CY 2018 were maximized.

observations.recommendation: 37)We recommended that the Chairman of the BAC and the BAC Secretariat strictly observe the requirements of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009, on the submission of copies of perfected contracts/purchase orders to the Auditor within five (5) days after execution/issuance thereof, to afford timely review and evaluation of the contract before the transaction is consummated.
observations.managementAction: POs were emailed by the BAC to the Audit Team. These were not supported with complete documentation. While there were Infrastructur e Contracts which were not submitted on time.

observations.recommendation: 38) Provide the necessary support in enjoining the barangays to establish their own MRFs and to fully implement waste segregation at source; 39) Strictly enforce and intensify the information campaign on the no-littering policy among the residents; and; 40) Consider embarking on effective projects and programs to support the LGUs ecological solid waste management program.
observations.managementAction: The LGU is on the process of finding lot for this purpose. Information campaign was conducted by the LGU. The LCPC had identified programs and activities for the protection of children’s rights and welfare in the Annual Work and Financial Plan which was included in the Annual Budget. The LGU is not yet fully-compliance d with the 5 basic environmental requirements per RA No. 9003. The site for the establishment of MRF in the barangays is the main problem.
observations.reason: Not Implemented Due to unavailable site for dump site

observations.recommendation: 42)We recommended for the immediate settlement/liquidation of the cash advances by the accountable officers. Demand letters should be issued by the Accountant to the Accountable Officers and enforce the sanctions that are available to the LGU for the collection of the advances. 43)Also, we recommended that management stops the practice of granting cash advances to elected officials for special/other purposes. Cash advances shall be allowed for their official travels only.
observations.managementAction: Refer to No. 32. The Municipal Mayor was still granted special cash advance which remained unliquidated to date.
observations.reason: Management is unwilling to follow regulations. The Municipal Mayor was still granted special cash advance which remained unliquidated to date.

observations.recommendation: 44)We recommended that management, through the Municipal Treasurer, returns the unexpended PDAF amounting P804,749.45 to the National Treasury.
observations.managementAction: This was returned to BTR already.

observations.recommendation: 46) Consistently adhere to the mandates under Section 89 of PD 1445, COA Circular 97-002 and other related rules and regulations specially those pertaining to the grant, utilization & liquidation of cash advances. 47) Grant cash advances only when previous one have been settled and duly accounted for in the books as certified by the Municipal Accountant pursuant to the pertinent provisions of COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012, Prescribing the Revised Guidelines & Documentary Requirements for Common Government Transactions. Send demands letters to all accountable officers requiring urgent liquidation or settlement of outstanding cash advances failure of which should cause the withholding of salaries and other claims due them equivalent to the noted unsettled amount in case such demand is unheeded.
observations.managementAction: Existing regulations on grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances was not followed. Demand letters were sent by the Municipal Accountant. The concerned officials remained unwilling to follow regulations. However, some partial actions: Partial settlement of advances was thru deduction from payrolls. Demand letters were sent by the Municipal Accountant. Some advances remained unlocated.

observations.recommendation: Refer to recommendation No. 33 above.
observations.managementAction: Implementation and liquidation of funds transferred from NGAs were already maximized in CY 2018 although there were still some projects for completion. Return of unutilized funds were already done.

observations.recommendation: Refer to recommendation No. 37 above.
observations.managementAction: POs were emailed by the BAC to the Audit Team. These were not supported with complete documentation; while there were Infrastructure Contracts which were not submitted on time.

observations.recommendation: Refer to recommendation No. 38 above.
observations.managementAction: The LGU is on the process of finding lot for this purpose. Information campaign was conducted by the LGU. The LGU is not yet fully-complianc e with the 5 basic environmental requirements per RA No. 9003. The site for the establishment of MRF in the barangays is the main problem.
observations.reason: Not Implemented Due to unavailable site for dump site

observations.recommendation: Refer to recommendation No. 29 and 30 above.
observations.managementAction: Reconciling items were being located; some remained unrecorded.
observations.reason: Reconciling items pertaining to unlocated transactions were not recorded for lack of basis.

observations.recommendation: Refer to recommendation No. 31 and 32 above.
observations.managementAction: Partial settlement of cash advances was thru deductions from payrolls; some documents were unlocated.
observations.reason: Some documents were not found yet.

observations.recommendation: 47) Grant cash advances only when previous one have been settled and duly accounted for in the books as certified by the Municipal Accountant pursuant to the pertinent provisions of COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012. Send demand letters to all accountable officers requiring urgent liquidation or settlement of outstanding cash advances failure of which should cause the withholding of salaries and other claims due them equivalent to the noted unsettled amount in case such demand is unheeded.
observations.managementAction: Demand letters were sent by the Municipal Accountant. Some regulations implemented; partial compliance.

observations.recommendation: 48) The LCE should instruct the Municipal Assessor to coordinate with and request from the Municipal Accountant, the list of all building and land improvements constructed/acquired by the Municipality. Conduct ocular inspection of these properties to validate actual existence utilization and condition for issuance of DRPs. Preparing and issuance of DPs prior to issuance of title should be prioritized. Preparation of Property Ledger Cards for all properties of the LGU should also be enforced to facilitate validation and reconciliation of the actual existence and utilization of all properties and equipment with accounting records.
observations.managementAction: As of December 31, 2018 there were two properties which remained not titled in the name of the LGU. Processing of title is, however, ongoing. Preparation of Property Ledger Cards for all properties of the LGU was being prioritized.
observations.reason: The LGU is in the process of titling said lots.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Chairman of the
Inventory Committee to properly conduct the physical count of LGU properties and
submit a complete report thereof. All unserviceable properties should be disposed of
following the procedures outlined in COA Circular 89-296.
Also, the Municipal Accountant and the Inventory Committee shall reconcile
their reports and records to settle discrepancies and bring the balances of the PPE
accounts in order.
During the exit conference, the designated Chairman of the Inventory Committee
and the Municipal Accountant agreed to adhere to the recommendations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer
to:
1) stop releasing DVs to payees and other persons and require the payees to
sign the DVs in the designated area within the premises of the Municipal
Treasurer’s Office;
2) exert thorough efforts to search and trace the missing disbursement
vouchers, otherwise, all unrecorded paid disbursements will be disallowed
in audit;
3) Maintain Check Register to record the date and names of claimants of
checks issued.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the LGU’s focal persons
of programs and projects funded by National Government Agencies to fast-track its
implementation, otherwise, return the funds to the source agency if the project cannot
be implemented. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant coordinate
with each project focal person on the immediate submission of liquidation documents
to the source agencies.
During the exit conference, the focal persons of the programs and projects justified
the delay on the implementation of the projects, however, they agreed to follow the
recommendations.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the Disbursement
Voucher to return the unused amounts of completed projects/programs to the source
National Government Agency, or to the Bureau of Treasury if the source agency can
no longer be traced and to return to the LGU concerned any amount due them.
During our exit conference, the Municipal Accountant and focal persons of the
projects/programs agreed to follow the recommendations.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) submit
justifications why Qorban Construction and Supply was not declared ineligible but
was awarded with five new contracts despite the entity’s non-disclosure in its
eligibility documents all previously awarded contracts.
We also recommended that the BAC and TWG review thoroughly the
eligibility documents submitted by interested bidders to detect any
misrepresentations and declare as eligible only those who strictly comply with the
provisions of RA 9184.
During the exit conference, the BAC Chairman was not present, however, a reply
to the AOM justifying the error was received by the Audit Team. She committed to adhere
to the recommendations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Planning
Development Officer to implement fully the development projects identified in the
Annual Investment Plan of the LGU for the current year pursuant to pertinent
provision of DILG-DBM JMC 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.
We also recommended that the Municipal Development Council re-evaluate
the necessity of the unimplemented projects in previous years and re-appropriate the
funds for other more relevant and currently-needed projects.
The Municipal Planning and Development Officer was not able to attend the exit
conference, however, a written reply to the AOM was received by the Audit Team re-
assuring the full implementation of LGUs development projects in the ensuing year.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal
Treasurer agreed to coordinate with each other and see to it that the remitted taxes
for the month tally with the taxes withheld per books.
Also, the unaccounted discrepancy should be given utmost attention and
should be adjusted immediately if necessary or remit the amount to the BIR if
determined to be included in the amount due to avoid additional incurrence of
surcharges.
Lastly, we recommended and the Municipal Accountant and Municipal
Treasurer as the persons primarily responsible for the recording and settlement of
the LGU’s tax liabilities agreed to immediately settle the amount due to BIR to avoid
incurrence of additional interest, be more religious in paying LGU taxes on or before
the due date and check the remittances made by their staff against the returns to
ascertain whether the amount paid is actually the amount due.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the LGU officials concerned
to:
1.) prepare the Program of Work and Detailed Estimates for the repair of the
defects on the project and the pipe connections from the stub out to the
individual households;
2.) undertake the repair of the defects on the project using the retention money
due to the contractor;
3.) appropriate from the 20% Development Funds the amount for the pipe
connections from the stub out to the individual households based on the Program of
Work;
4.) make the project operational in CY 2018.
During the exit conference, the MPDC was not present, however, a reply to the AOM
was submitted stating that the audit recommendations shall be followed.

We recommended that the Municipal DRRMO maximize the utilization of the
LDRRMF in the Trust Fund by including in the LDRRMF Investment Plan
programs, projects and activities which will ensure readiness of the LGU in times of
calamity.
During the exit conference, the LGU officials concerned agreed to follow the
recommendations.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer ensure that the appropriate
bank account is charged for disaster-related expenses out of the current year’s
LDRRMF. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant make the
necessary accounting entries to rectify the same and restore the correct balance of CY
2017 LDRRM fund.
During the exit conference, the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal
Treasurer agreed to correct the error committed.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: (1) defer disbursement of LGU
funds on the lot which is not legally owned by the municipality to avoid possible
wastage of funds and disallowance in audit, and (2) closely monitor the immediate
transfer of the title to the LGU considering that advance payments has been made to
the heirs, otherwise the same maybe disallowed in audit.
During the exit conference, the LGU officials concerned justified that the
processing of the transfer of ownership of the lot to the LGU is already with the court. They
promised to adhere to the recommendations.

We recommended that the Human Resource and Management Officer
reconcile their records with the GSIS and communicate immediately to the said
agency any necessary change in the employees’ employment status to update the
records of the GSIS and tally with the LGU records on premium contributions.
We also recommended that the Agency Authorized Officer (AO) furnish
monthly all payroll preparer with copies of the GSIS billing to ensure that the correct
amount of monthly premium contributions and loan amortizations deducted in the
payroll are based on the said GSIS billing.
Lastly, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconcile monthly the
actual deductions in the payroll against the GSIS Billing and against the amount
remitted to avoid discrepancies therein.
During the exit conference, the Municipal Accountant justified that some
discrepancies for loans were due to the payments by the employees thru the Municipal
Treasurer’s Office. However, they agreed to verify the amount of under remittances and
reconcile with GSIS.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure that payments of JO
wages are supported with the Contracts of Service of each JO to preclude the
suspension or disallowance of transactions during post-audit.
During the exit conference, the LGU officials concerned agreed to adhere to the
recommendations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor fast track the acquisition of land
for MRF and the transfer of ownership of the land at the final disposal site for the
establishment of Materials Recovery Facility and Sanitary Landfill.
During the exit conference, the LGU incharge in the Solid Waste Management and
other LGU Officials justified that the problem lies on the lack of lot for the establishment
of the MRF, nevertheless, they agreed to follow the recommendations.

We recommended that the Local School Board Council incorporate in its
budget programs and activities intended for Early Childhood Care Development.
During the exit conference, the Local School Board Council agreed to follow the
audit recommendations and incorporate in its budget programs and activities intended for
ECCD.

We recommended that the Mayor as the Chairman of the Municipal Council
for the Protection of Children (LCPC) require its members to identify programs,
projects and activities for the protection of children’s rights and welfare by preparing
the Council’s Annual Work and Financial Plan for inclusion in the Annual Budget of
the Municipality. The total appropriation of said programs/projects and activities,
which can be attributed from PPAs of other departments, shall be equivalent to one
percent (1%) of the IRA in the Annual Budget.
During the exit conference, the MSWDO in behalf of the whole Local Council
committed to identify programs, projects and activities in their Annual Work and Financial
Plan for the protection of children for inclusion in the Annual Budget.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer submit immediately all paid but
unrecorded disbursement vouchers to the Municipal Accountant’s Office for
recording in the LGUs books of accounts. These disbursement vouchers and its
supporting documents must be submitted to COA immediately for audit to avoid
suspensions or disallowances in audit. Also, ensure that all DVs are supported with
complete supporting documents and bear the signatures of all the approving
officials before checks are issued to the claimants to avoid incurring problems on
delayed recording and loss of documents.
We also recommend that the Municipal Accountant record the reconciling
items in the Bank Reconciliation Statements, especially those based on the bank
statements.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor strictly enforce the liquidation of
all cash advances at the end of the year to protect government funds from possible
loss or misuse.
We further recommend that Municipal Accountant withhold the salaries of
all employees who continuously fail to settle their outstanding cash advances and
refrain from granting additional cash advances to those with previous unliquidated
balances.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary adjusting
entries to recognize the appropriate Property, Plant and Equipment account to
which the cost of these construction materials pertain.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineer
and the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer to accelerate the
implementation of projects funded by the National Government Agencies (NGAs)
and to initiate the return of the funds to the National Treasury if the purpose can no
longer be attained.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Assessor to
facilitate the submission of the lacking documents to prove that: (a) the purchased
lot was free from any encumbrances; (b) the payee was the rightful owner of the
purchased lot, and; (c) the sale was duly registered with the Register of Deeds.
Likewise, require the Provincial Appraisal Committee to submit the BIR Zonal
Valuation to support the established purchase price.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineer to
fast-track the implementation of development projects programmed in the Annual
Investment Plan of the LGU in order to fully utilize the 20% development fund
during the year in accordance with the purpose for which it was intended.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the MDRRM Officer to
integrate in the LDRRMF Investment Plan the programs/projects and activities to
be funded from the unexpended LDRRMF of prior years in the Trust Fund to
provide assurance that prior authority has been obtained. Also, we recommend that
the Municipal Mayor submit the approval of the LDRRMF Council and SB on the
purchase of the heavy equipment using the Trust Fund.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal DRRMF
Officer to implement the programs, projects and activities in the annual LDRRMF
Investment Plan in order to maximize the utilization of the LDRRM fund and
achieve fully the LGU’s objective of enhancing the municipality’s disaster
preparedness and response capabilities.

We recommend that the MDRRM Officer submit the Report on Sources and
Utilization of LDRRMF monthly and include in the report all information required
in Annex B of COA Circular 2012-002 to enable the concerned offices to conduct
timely evaluation of the LDRRMF utilization.

We recommend that the Local School Board include as topmost priority the
improvement of school facilities and other priority programs/projects/activities for
the use of Special Education Fund and maximize the use of the fund in accordance
with the intended purpose in order to obtain the desired benefits.

We recommend that the Chairman of the BAC and the BAC Secretariat
strictly observe the requirements of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12,
2009, on the submission of copies of perfected contracts/purchase orders to the
Auditor within five (5) days after execution/issuance thereof, to afford timely review
and evaluation of the contract before the transaction is consummated.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Engineer sign,
the Certificate of Acceptance one year after the completion of the project or after
the one-year defects liability period have lapsed. Only the Certificate of Completion
should be issued upon completion of the project together with the requirement for
the posting of the warranty security.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: (a) provide the necessary
support in enjoining the barangays to establish their own MRFs and to fully
implement waste segregation at source; (b) strictly enforce and intensify the
information campaign on the no-littering policy among the residents; and (c)
consider embarking on effective projects and programs to support the LGUs
ecological solid waste management program.
